scenes.text:
The ground drops away into a ravine and the road gives out. Below, the old iron mines still breathe steam into the night.
You found the lower entrance — a ledge wide enough for one person.
Mines are trouble. Mines are also truth.
This is where the king’s iron used to come from. This is where prisoners used to disappear.

choices.text: Hunt the ledge for another way.
Turn back while you still can.

scenes.text:
You find a rusted cart and a single rail. Gravity is the only engine left.
The tunnel opens into a chamber where the miners carved names into the rock. One name is recent. "Elara."
A draft pulls at your clothes from a shaft below.
Something in the dark is already aware of you.

You find a dry bunk, a journal, and a miner’s lamp that still works.
The last entry says: "She asked questions about the old kings. Then she went through the lower door."
There is also a pickaxe. Heavy. Useful.

choices.text: Take the journal and lamp, then descend.
Leave it. Noise will kill you down there.

scenes.text:
The lower tunnels are stone veins in the mountain’s marrow.
You step into a wide chamber lined with carved tiles. Symbols match the doorway in the hill.
Someone was here. Witness marks are still fresh on the stone.
A passage opens to a black pool. The water is still.
A shape moves under the surface.

You move along the ledge. The wind is the worst enemy here.
Halfway around the ravine you find a shrine nailed to the rock. A bit of ribbon. A coin. A note: "She looked both ways before she jumped, but the sky looked back."
You are not the first to hunt her.
You may be the last.

choices.text: Take the shrine coin as a token.

scenes.text:
The tiles are a map. They show the gate, the kingdom, and a fourth place — a tower beneath the water.
The story is incomplete in the kingdom’s records. The missing page is here, chiseled in secret.
The missing page names the prisoner who knows the way: the spymaster.

choices.text: Remember the map. Descend to the pool.

scenes.text:
The water is warm, although it shouldn’t be.
In the reflection you see a face you do not recognize wearing your clothes.
A hand breaks the surface and drops a key on the stone.
Then it is gone again.

The creature is pale. Too many joints. It watches you with the patience of something that was chained here before the castle had stones.
It does not attack. It waits to see what you will do.
The spymaster’s voice is stuck in its throat like a second ghost.

choices.text: Command it with the signet ring.

scenes.text:
The creature accepts the offering and steps aside. The passage behind it is carved with a single sentence: "The crown forgot. The gate remembers."
A second corridor leads upward toward a tower.
You have earned the guardian’s silence. That is worth more than a soldier’s oath.

You strike, but the creature fights only to survive. It leaves you bleeding on the stone and vanishes into the dark.
You survive. The gate ahead does not open.
